title: frangula vulgaris
intro: Frangula vulgaris, also known as buckthorn or alder buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia. The plant's bark contains a compound called anthraquinone, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Frangula vulgaris has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, constipation, and liver and gallbladder issues, due to its ability to stimulate bowel movements and improve liver function. Frangula vulgaris can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
